title: ITLOS – Nippon Foundation Capacity-Building and Training Programme 2026–2027 (Fully Funded Law of the Sea Fellowship in Germany)
summary: Key Details Programme: ITLOS Nippon Foundation Capacity-Building and Training Programme on Dispute Settlement under UNCLOS ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Location: Hamburg, Germany Duration: July 2026 March 2027 (9 months, full-time) Funding: Fully funded (travel, accommodation, medical insurance, and monthly allowance) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Application Deadline: 6 March 2026 Eligibility: Junior to mid-level government officials and researchers, mainly from developing countries ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Age Requirement: 25 40 years Language: English and French (programme mainly conducted in English) Overview In cooperation with the Nippon Foundation , the Tribunal runs an annual capacity-building and training programme on dispute settlement under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea ( UNCLOS ). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The full-time programme, which runs for nine months and begins in July each year, takes place at the seat of the Tribunal in Hamburg, Germany. Contingent upon the availability of funds, a call for applications is generally announced each year during the month of January or February. The programme is aimed at junior to mid-level government officials and researchers mainly from developing countries who are currently working on issues related to the law of the sea, maritime law or dispute settlement. It provides participants with a unique opportunity to develop their legal skills and deepen their practical knowledge of dispute settlement in the law of the sea under UNCLOS. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); All participants costs, including travel, accommodation, medical insurance and a monthly allowance, are covered by the Nippon Foundation. Flyer Lectures A series of lectures takes place throughout the nine months of the programme. Lectures are generally held over the course of two days, offering the fellows the possibility of close debate with the lecturers. The faculty of lecturers is drawn from Judges of the Tribunal, Registry officials, United Nations and international organizations, universities, law firms and other relevant institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); At the beginning of the programme, the fellows take part in the four-week Summer Academy of the International Foundation for the Law of the Sea . This course focuses on law of the sea and maritime law and is attended by around 40 young professionals from around the world working in the fields of law of the sea and maritime law. Some examples of lectures given during recent programmes are: Dispute settlement under UNCLOS The mechanism for the settlement of disputes under Part XV of UNCLOS with a special focus on ITLOS Contentious and advisory proceedings Giving evidence before international courts and tribunals Provisional measures Prompt release International organizations and the law of the sea The role of international organizations in the implementation of UNCLOS The work of the Division for Ocean Affairs and the Law of the Sea, Office of Legal Affairs, United Nations The mandate and work of the Commission on the Limits of the Continental Shelf under UNCLOS The interaction between the International Seabed Authority and the Tribunal Current issues related to the law of the sea Law of maritime delimitation Law of the sea and human rights The Arctic and UNCLOS The establishment of the limits of the continental shelf Fisheries management Marine scientific research Marine biological diversity of areas beyond national jurisdiction International environmental law General principles of international environmental law UNCLOS and the regional seas programmes The precautionary principle and the protection of the marine environment Climate change and the law of the sea Biodiversity and protection of ecosystems Maritime law lecture series Maritime security Ship registration Vessel pollution International law lecture series Sources of international law law of international organizations State responsibility Law of treaties Training Training sessions and workshops are arranged in order to enhance the practical and professional skills of the fellows. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); The programme offers training in negotiation and mediation. Workshops are organized to prepare the fellows for their potential role as agents, counsel, or legal advisers in cases before international courts and tribunals. The tools used for this purpose include exercises in drafting documents relating to cases, attendance at oral proceedings at the Tribunal, the International Court of Justice or the Permanent Court of Arbitration, and presentation skills using audio-visual tools. Collaborating institutes An integral part of the training and capacity-building programme on dispute settlement under UNCLOS is the cooperative arrangement with Max Planck Foundation for International Peace and the Rule of Law in Heidelberg, Germany, and the Norwegian Centre for the Law of the Sea in Troms , Norway. Cooperation is undertaken in the following areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Max Planck Foundation for International Peace and the Rule of Law in Heidelberg, Germany: training on conflict resolution and visits to the library of the Max Planck Institute for Comparative Public Law and International Law . Norwegian Centre for the Law of the Sea in Troms , Norway: discussions and training on the law of the sea and visits to relevant institutions. Study visits Group visits of between two and five days complement the knowledge gained and training given in dispute settlement and issues/topics related to the law of the sea. Accompanied by a legal officer, the fellows are given exclusive access to experts from the institutions and have the chance to attend hearings or meetings. In previous years, the following visits have been organized for the fellows: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Belgium: Brussels European Commission s Directorate-General for Maritime Affairs and Fisheries Germany: Berlin, Bremen, Cuxhaven, Hamburg, Hanover, Kiel Alfred Wegener Institut Federal Coastguard Federal Institute for Geosciences and Natural Resources Federal Maritime Hydrographic Agency GEOMAR Helmholtz Center for Ocean Research Kiel Hamburg Port Authority Maritime Safety and Security Centre Italy: Rome Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Switzerland: Geneva The United Nations Office at Geneva Geneva Center for International Dispute Settlement The Netherlands: The Hague International Court of Justice International Criminal Court Permanent Court of Arbitration United Kingdom: London International Maritime Organization International Oil Pollution Compensation Funds OSPAR Commission Research Research on law of the sea disputes and other ocean matters relevant to governments and institutions of the participants is an integral part of the training. After a training session on legal research methodology, the fellows undertake research using the library facilities of the Tribunal. Participants undertake their research under the guidance and supervision of the Registry. Participants have also the opportunity to refer questions concerning their research topics to the Judges of the Tribunal. The results of the research are presented to the Judges of the Tribunal at the end of the programme. Eligibility Applicants must be between the ages of 25 and 40. Applicants must have a first university degree in law and demonstrate a capacity to undertake independent scientific research, study and training. Applicants must be: Junior to mid-level government officials from an administration or government agency, dealing with ocean affairs or maritime matters as well as legal issues relating thereto; or Researchers from an institute (academic or government) dealing with ocean-related or maritime matters as well as legal issues relating thereto. Applicants must indicate the research topic which they intend to pursue during the programme. The research topic should address legal issues of relevance to the State and institutions of the participants regarding the implementation of UNCLOS, in particular Part XV thereof. The administration or institute nominating the applicant must indicate on the nomination form the tasks performed by the applicant and explain the extent to which the training programme will benefit the institution/State concerned. Applicants must have an excellent knowledge of one of the official languages of the Tribunal (English and French) and a working knowledge of the other language. The lectures and activities of the Programme are mainly held in English. Procedure The following documents must be submitted in order for an application to be processed: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Fellowship Application Form to be duly completed, signed and submitted by the applicant. Nomination Form to be duly completed, signed and directly submitted by the nominating agency. The submitted form must be typed. In the case of government officials, the nominating agency should be a relevant governmental body. For researchers, the nominating agency may be the institute or academic department of the university to which they are affiliated. Nominating agencies are invited to submit two copies of the Nomination Form: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); One scanned version of the signed original; and One electronic version. The Nomination Form should preferably be sent by e-mail to training@itlos.org , by fax to: +49 40 35607-245, or by post to: Office of the Registrar International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea Am Internationalen Seegerichtshof 1 22609 Hamburg Germany ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Conditional acceptance to the programme Admitted applicants must submit the following documents: A medical certificate issued by a duly qualified physician stating that they are in good health and fit to complete the entire programme (from July 2026 until March 2027). The certificate must indicate that the admitted applicant is able to travel inside Germany and overseas. A certificate issued by the nominating agency indicating that the candidate is granted permission to travel to Hamburg and to participate in the full nine-month programme from July 2026 to March 2027. Final admission to the programme will be contingent upon submission of the medical and permission certificates. Admitted applicants must submit a copy of the certificates within two weeks of announcing the admission. The original certificates must be submitted on the first day of the programme and must be in either English or French or accompanied by a certified translation. Participants costs, including travel, accommodation, medical insurance and a monthly subsistence allowance, are covered by the Nippon Foundation. Participants are responsible for obtaining a visa for Germany for the period of the programme at their own cost, although assistance may be provided in order to facilitate the visa application process. During the programme, the fellows become part of the ITLOS community and are fully integrated in the life of the Registry for nine months. They interact with staff, Judges and participants of the other training programmes run at the Tribunal. As ITLOS alumni, the fellows join a growing network of young professionals from around the world working in the field. Fellows are provided with a fully equipped office with computer, printer and internet connection for the duration of the programme. Fellows have access to the Tribunal s specialized Library, which houses a comprehensive collection on the law of the sea and related subjects, such as maritime law, environmental law, ocean affairs, coastal management, international courts and tribunals, international organizations, dispute settlement, arbitration, and general issues of public international law. Accommodation in the vicinity of the Tribunal is provided in studio apartments, with a bedroom/sitting area, kitchen and bathroom. During their first weeks in Hamburg, the fellows are introduced to life in Germany and given German language classes. Language training in French may also be provide For further information please contact: The ITLOS-Nippon Programme Coordinator International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea Am Internationalen Seegerichtshof 1 22609 Hamburg Germany Tel.: (+49) 40 35607-243 Fax: (+49) 40 35607-245 training@itlos.org Source: https://www.itlos.org/en/main/the-registry/training/itlos-nippon-foundation-capacity-building-and-training-programme/ The post ITLOS – Nippon Foundation Capacity-Building and Training Programme 2026–2027 (Fully Funded Law of the Sea Fellowship in Germany) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AI Skills 4 Women: Free AI Course by Founderz × Microsoft with Official Credential
summary: The AI Skills 4 Women program is a free, fully online course designed to equip women with practical artificial intelligence skills no technical background required. This initiative is delivered by Founderz in collaboration with Microsoft to help close the gender gap in AI and technology. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); If you re looking to enter the AI space or strengthen your digital skills, this short but impactful program is worth exploring. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What You ll Learn Participants will gain foundational and practical knowledge in: AI Fundamentals Practical AI Applications Prompt Engineering AI Ethics AI Cybersecurity Data Analytics Leadership Mentorship Skills ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The course is structured to be accessible, even for beginners with no prior technical experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Program Details Duration: 3 Hours Mode: 100% Online Credential: Official Microsoft Certificate Additional Benefit: Opportunity to win AI scholarships The short format makes it ideal for students, professionals, entrepreneurs, and career switchers who want to quickly build AI literacy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Who Should Enroll? This program is open to: Women interested in technology and AI Students exploring digital careers Professionals looking to upskill Entrepreneurs integrating AI into their work Career switchers entering tech No prior coding or technical background is required. Why This Matters Women remain underrepresented in AI and emerging technologies. Programs like AI Skills 4 Women aim to increase participation, confidence, and leadership in the digital economy. By completing this course, participants gain both technical awareness and strategic insight into how AI is transforming industries worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); How to Enroll Enroll for free and begin your AI learning journey here . For more OFY short courses , click here The post AI Skills 4 Women: Free AI Course by Founderz × Microsoft with Official Credential appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: LEAP Advance Leadership Development Program 2026: Elevate Your Impact in Higher Education
summary: The LEAP Advance Leadership Development Program is an intensive leadership initiative designed to support higher education professionals in strengthening their leadership capacity and advancing their influence within academic institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Delivered in partnership with the Asian Pacific Americans in Higher Education (APAHE) , the program prepares participants to lead with confidence through an Asian and Pacific Islander (API) cultural lens. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This program is ideal for professionals seeking greater visibility, executive presence, and leadership effectiveness in colleges and universities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About LEAP Advance LEAP Advance is a structured leadership development experience tailored specifically for professionals working in higher education. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Through culturally responsive leadership training and mentorship, participants gain the tools needed to navigate institutional structures, enhance executive presence, and expand their professional influence. The program centers leadership development within the context of Asian and Pacific Islander identities, perspectives, and lived experiences, fostering both personal growth and systemic impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Curriculum Highlights The LEAP Advance curriculum is designed to provide both practical leadership skills and identity-based empowerment. Key components include: Culturally Responsive Leadership Development Participants explore leadership frameworks that integrate cultural identity and institutional dynamics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); One-on-One and Small Group Mentoring Structured mentoring provides individualized support and professional guidance. Mock Interviewing Practice and Executive Presence Hands-on sessions help participants refine communication, confidence, and interview readiness for senior roles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Engagement with Senior Higher Education Leaders Interactive sessions with senior administrators and college presidents offer insight into executive-level leadership. Key Outcomes Participants in LEAP Advance can expect: Enhanced leadership capacity within academic institutions Increased self-awareness of cultural leadership identity Strengthened executive presence and communication skills Expanded professional network within higher education Greater visibility and readiness for senior leadership roles This program is particularly impactful for professionals aiming to advance into administrative, executive, or institutional leadership positions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Why You Should Apply If you are a higher education professional seeking to: Strengthen your leadership competencies Lead authentically through a culturally grounded lens Expand your professional network Prepare for senior-level roles in academia LEAP Advance offers a targeted pathway to elevate your influence and long-term career trajectory. How to Apply Interested applicants should visit the official program page through the organizing institution to access the application details and submission link . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Apply here Discover more opportunities here The post LEAP Advance Leadership Development Program 2026: Elevate Your Impact in Higher Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
